summary: Iteration 16 backend smoke — FAZ 7 (İmalat Planı 18-col CRUD + product autofill + derived fields). Created pytest suite with 16 tests, all pass. Verified: seq_no auto-increment, planned_delivery = payment_date+21 (both on create and on PUT payment_date change), product autofill (collection/color/purchase_price/name → description), manufacturer_name lookup by id, delay_days (negative / zero / positive), qty_diff_pct (20.0 for 120/100, None when delivered_qty=0 — fix verified, no more -100), 404 on unknown PUT/DELETE, search by model_no, manufacturer_id filter, /production-plan/collections distinct (product + plan merged, sorted, no dupes), delete removal verified via GET list, product autofill reflects latest values after PUT /products.

minor.issue: seq_no is computed as count_documents({}) + 1. After DELETEs, newly inserted rows will reuse the seq_no of previously-deleted rows, producing duplicates. Prefer max(seq_no)+1 or a dedicated counters collection.

product_id is in the 'allowed' update set but no re-autofill runs on PUT. If UI lets user change product on an existing row, the row's collection/price/color/product_description will not refresh from the new product. Consider replaying the autofill block when product_id changes (and empty fields are to be refilled) or document as client-side responsibility.

Accepts arbitrary dict (payload: dict) with only allow-list filtering — no Pydantic validation, so type errors (e.g. order_qty='abc') can persist strings into the doc and break _compute_derived downstream. Suggest Pydantic PlanRowUpdate with Optional fields.

Empty-payload row accepted with order_qty=0, price=0. Acceptable for UX (spreadsheet rows added empty and filled later), but there's no server-side minimum (e.g. at least model_no) — flag for product decision.

action_items: Change seq_no generation from count_documents+1 to max(seq_no)+1 (or use a counters doc) to avoid duplicates after deletes.
Optional: re-run product autofill on PUT /production-plan when product_id changes, or document that the UI must resend collection/color/price fields.
Optional: replace raw dict payload on PUT /production-plan/{id} with a Pydantic update model to enforce types on order_qty/delivered_qty/cut_qty.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/production_plan.py::create_plan_row uses count_documents+1 for seq_no → non-unique after deletes; under concurrent POSTs this is also race-prone.

routes/production_plan.py::update_plan_row accepts plain dict with only allow-list filter; no Pydantic validation. A string order_qty would survive and blow up _compute_derived math silently (returns None via except).

routes/production_plan.py::_compute_derived: planned_delivery is only auto-computed when doc.get('planned_delivery') is falsy. That's correct on CREATE. On UPDATE the endpoint explicitly recomputes when payment_date is in payload & planned_delivery is not — verified via test. Note: if the client sends both payment_date AND planned_delivery='', the empty string will be overwritten correctly. OK.
routes/production_plan.py::qty_diff_pct: now returns None when delivered_qty<=0 instead of -100%. Matches iteration 16 fix. Confirmed.

routes/products.py::create_product: correctly stores collection/purchase_price/color with safe float coercion. update_product does a blind $set of the entire payload dict — no whitelist — which means a client could overwrite 'id', 'created_at', etc. Not a FAZ 7 regression but worth flagging.
seed_data_creation: One TEST_Manufacturer_* vendor and one TEST_Prod_* product; multiple production_plan rows — all removed via fixture teardown (module scope).
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iteration16_production_plan.py covers all FAZ 7 acceptance points for /api/production-plan (CRUD, search, filter, collections, autofill-from-product) and for POST/PUT /api/products with new fields (collection, color, purchase_price). Uses module-scoped fixtures with teardown. Safe to rerun. If re-running, note vendor endpoint returns vendor id in different shapes — fixture probes nested 'vendor.id' too.
rca of the issue: N/A — all feature acceptance tests pass. Minor concerns logged above (seq_no uniqueness, PUT payload validation, product_id re-autofill on update) are non-blocking.
